back: Red Delicious
Flavor: Mildly sweet, low acidity, often described as sugary but not very complex.
Appearance: Deep, uniform red skin; elongated, conical shape with five prominent bumps at the bottom.
Bred/Created: Discovered as a chance seedling in Iowa, USA in the late 19th century; originally called “Hawkeye,” later propagated for its color and sweetness rather than flavor complexity.
Granny Smith
Flavor: Tart, crisp, and tangy with a refreshing acidity.
Appearance: Bright green skin, sometimes with small white speckles; firm, round shape.
Bred/Created: Discovered in Australia in 1868 by Maria Ann Smith from a chance seedling; propagated for its tart flavor and excellent storage qualities.
Gala
Flavor: Sweet and mildly aromatic, low acidity.
Appearance: Orange-red striped over yellow background; small to medium size.
Bred/Created: Developed in New Zealand in the 1930s by crossing Kidd's Orange Red and Golden Delicious.
Fuji
Flavor: Very sweet, crisp, juicy, low acidity.
Appearance: Large, round, red-orange with yellow-green undertones.
Bred/Created: Developed in Japan in the 1930s-1960s by crossing Red Delicious and Ralls Janet.
Honeycrisp
Flavor: Sweet-tart, exceptionally juicy and crisp.
Appearance: Red-orange blush over yellow background; medium to large size.
Bred/Created: Developed by the University of Minnesota in the 1960s–1990s from a cross of Keepsake and an unknown variety.
Golden Delicious
Flavor: Sweet, mellow, low acidity.
Appearance: Yellow-gold skin, smooth and medium to large size.
Bred/Created: Discovered as a chance seedling in West Virginia, USA, in the late 19th century; propagated for its sweet flavor and versatility.
